council_result: S110 batch PDR (.claude/plans/2026-06-26_PDR_S110_Batch_E_plus_B_plus_D.md). 5 priorities from owner live playtest: P1 win-points 786->1500 (+tier-step 262->500, exact-thirds); P2 uniform spark speed (min=max=12, keep rngRange draw); P3 codex keep player-avatar visible above backdrop; P4 Batch B Helga walk-to-target+melee (v12->13, HIGH); P5 Batch D wire 3 imagen art keepers (offline matte->atlas->render-swap, lazy-load, no bump). Council R1 Grok-4.20-reasoning + Gemini-2.5-pro + Opus PRIME-AUDIT, HIGH confidence, no SPLIT. ADOPTED: P2 keep-the-draw discipline; P4 walk-speed ~=chewer (catchable, not undercut) + bounded acquisition radius (anti-kite); P4 two-commit split (substrate+WALK, then chase AI); P5 graceful fallback to procedural rig until atlas loads. REJECTED on audit: Grok fixed-point/client-extrapolation integrator (contradicts proven float-Verlet determinism + no-client-physics arch); Grok seed-pinned-art (offline-baked, no runtime gen). CARRY-FORWARD (logged, Gemini quality flag): proper Helga walk-cycle (Veo loop/multi-pose) once veo/imagen reference-conditioning works in this auth -- first-pass P5 static sprite slides while walking.

Build under cap. reflexion_entries_to_archive: S108-PLAN #verify-the-councils-confident-consensus-against-the-actual-architecture: a 3-way Council UNANIMOUSLY headlined a MANDATORY PROTOCOL_VERSION bump asserting the client predicts physics; PRIME-AUDIT refuted it against the code (SPARK client runs ZERO authoritative physics). LESSON: two models converging is NOT independent confirmation when they share a generic prior; verify confident consensus against the actual codebase before adopting.

S109-PLAN-A #host-only-field-needs-no-wire-bump-when-the-serializer-is-a-whitelist: adding Creature.poopyUntilTick needed ZERO save.ts changes because serializeCreature is a field WHITELIST. LESSON: read the serializer before assuming a new field forces a protocol bump.

S109-PLAN-A #the-unlock-hook-vocabulary-vs-the-final-gate-vocabulary: the unlock hook writes priority_state:'unlocked' but pdca-final-gate accepts only {approved,in_progress,completed}. FIX: reconcile priority_state to 'in_progress' after the hook mints unlock_source:user (the load-bearing field).

S110-BATCH #commit-and-push-is-not-the-same-as-deployed: all 5 S110 commits passed tsc + 1710 vitest + build-under-cap and pushed clean, but `gh run list` showed EVERY S110 deploy at startup_failure/0s/0-jobs while S109 succeeded. The bundle-cap memory's 'on shipped-but-not-working check gh run list FIRST' caught it. Root cause was NOT code/bundle/workflow-file (files valid + unchanged since the green Jun 26 run; Actions enabled): a private-repo GitHub Actions SPENDING-LIMIT/minutes cap (the ~35min/push Playwright e2e exhausted the 2000 free minutes) blocks ALL new runs (push AND manual workflow_dispatch) with 0 jobs. LESSON: 'done' = DEPLOYED, not just pushed-green; always verify the live deploy via `gh run list` at batch close, and a 0-job startup_failure that survives a manual dispatch = an account/billing block (owner action), not a code fix. Prevention: don't run a heavy e2e on every push on a minutes-capped private repo.

S110-BATCH #engage-the-pdr-lock-WHEN-you-present-not-after: the user said 'go' twice but the write-gate never minted unlock_source=user because glue_pdr_unlock only mints when a lock file (pdr-gate-lock / pdr-lock-<id>) exists at the approval prompt AND an in_progress priority exists — and I'd presented the PDR without engaging the lock first. LESSON: the lock is the 'awaiting approval' marker; create it (+ stage the in_progress priority) WHEN you present the PDR, so the user's very next 'go' mints the unlock. Also: a multi-priority batch must be ONE in_progress session-state entry (the S109 PLAN-A pattern) — separate per-priority entries each need their own unlock, which re-blocks at every priority boundary.

S110-P5 #border-connected-component-matte-beats-luma-key: imagen stills on a uniform white bg matte cleanly to transparent by removing ONLY border-connected near-white (scipy.ndimage.label + drop border-touching components), which preserves interior whites (Voltkin belly, Helga beer-foam/apron) and leaves NO square box — exactly the S106 failure mode a naive luma/white key caused (it punched holes in the #FFEB6B belly + left the box the owner hated). Verify the matte via a dark-bg preview composite BEFORE wiring. public/ assets are unbundled by construction (separate static files) so lazy-loading them is bundle-cap-free.
